summary: Scorching China>Rider and soaring Bird Song with tight SBD
blurb: The set delivers a blistering China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider segue and a powerful Bird Song jam. Jerry’s solos are inventive and Brent’s keyboards trippy, while Phil anchors with heavy bass. The only dip is the awkward "Don't Need Love" slot.
review: On June 10, 1984 at Cal Expo, the Dead fired on all cylinders. The first set opened strong with a dark‑voiced Bird Song that set the tone for a night of high‑energy improvisation. The second set’s highlight was the classic China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider transition – a seamless, soaring passage that showcased Jerry’s blazing solo work and Brent’s psychedelic keyboard textures. From there the band rode a powerful groove through He’s Gone, an extended The Other One‑style jam (though not on the official setlist), and a post‑drums Truckin' that kept the momentum rolling into Throwing Stones and a rousing Not Fade Away. Phil’s bass was “big, loud, and turned up,” plundering the low end with authority, while Mickey and Bill laid down solid, responsive drumming throughout. The lone blemish was the dated "Don't Need Love" which felt out of place amid an otherwise tight performance. Overall, the show captures a vibrant snapshot of the 1984 era – Jerry in rare form, Brent’s keyboards at their trippiest, and the rhythm section locked in.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings praise standout songs (Bird Song, China>Rider, He's Gone) and highlight Jerry’s inventive solos, Phil’s heavy bass, and Brent’s trippy keyboards, indicating overall positive sentiment. A few mixed notes note the weak "Don't Need Love" and occasional sloppiness, tempering enthusiasm but not outweighing the positives, resulting in a modest +0.8 adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Tight soundboard capture with good balance, minimal drop‑outs, and clear detail that showcases the standout China>Rider and Bird Song jams.

key_highlights: Blazing China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider segue that drives the second set.
Extended Bird Song jam with soaring guitar and trippy keyboards.
Post‑drums Truckin' maintains high energy into the encore.
"Don't Need Love" feels out of place, dragging the momentum briefly.

band_performance.Jerry: Creative, relentless solos described as outstanding and blazing; a few critics called the performance sloppy or felt he was underused, but overall his playing is inventive and a highlight of the night.
band_performance.Phil: Locked in with a heavy, loud bass that plunders the groove; multiple reviewers note his foundation as big, powerful, and bomb‑like throughout the show.
band_performance.Brent: Distinctive, trippy keyboard work praised for technical excellence; his toy‑piano clicks and atmospheric textures drive the jam sections.
band_performance.Billy: Solid drumming with a strong post‑drums Truckin' highlight; occasional impatience noted but overall rhythm section is tight and supportive.
